title: FEGA dataset metadata schema
description: Collection of Datafiles subject to a Policy for controlled-access distribution.

meta:sssomMappings.$comment: A FEGA dataset is a specific type of DCAT Dataset (HealthDCAT-AP non-public), focused on controlled-access biomedical data.

accessRights.title: Access rights
accessRights.description: Information that indicates whether the Dataset is publicly accessible, has access restrictions or is not public. In the case of EGA datasets, all are 'NON_PUBLIC': the dataset is not publicly available and access is subject to restrictions.

alternative.title: Alternative title
alternative.description: Alternative title for the Dataset. This property can be repeated for parallel language versions of the alternative title.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: An analytics distribution of the dataset. Publishers are encouraged to provide URLs pointing to document repositories where users can access or request associated resources such as technical reports of the dataset, quality measurements, usability indicators, etc. This property is particularly relevant for datasets that are not publicly accessible, as it allows data providers to share valuable information about the dataset's characteristics and quality without exposing the data itself.

applicableLegislation.title: Applicable legislation
applicableLegislation.description: The legislation that mandates the creation or management of the Dataset. As multiple legislations may apply to the resource the maximum cardinality is not limited.

hasCodeValues.title: Code values
hasCodeValues.description: A free-text description of any health classification, terminology, ontology, thesaurus, or coding system used in the dataset.
examples: Y59.0 - Viral vaccines causing adverse effects in therapeutic use (ICD-10 code)

hasCodingSystem.title: Coding system
hasCodingSystem.description: Coding systems in use (e.g., ICD-10-CM, DGRs, SNOMED CT). This property allows you to specify the coding systems used within your dataset. For example, if a dataset uses ICD-10 for disease classification, this property allows data users to search for datasets with the same coding system. As a machine-actionable property, it also facilitates automated processes, making dataset discovery more efficient. Specify the standardised coding systems used in the dataset (e.g., ICD-10-CM, SNOMED CT, DRGs).

conformsTo.title: Conforms to
conformsTo.description: An established standard or specification to which the described resource conforms.

contactPoint.title: Contact point
contactPoint.description: Contact information that can be used for sending comments about the Dataset. This property informs about a contact point (Individual, Organization, Location, Group) that can answer questions about the dataset.

creator.title: Creator
creator.description: An entity responsible for creating the Dataset.

meta:sssomMappings.$comment: EGA's runs contain datafiles. This method of dataset-file linkage is what is similar to the DCAT's distribution.
EGA's analyses contain datafiles. This method of dataset-file linkage is what is similar to the DCAT's distribution.
A Beacon 'externalUrl' is broader than the specific FEGA/healthdcatap 'documentation'.

hdab.title: Health Data Access Body
hdab.$comment: Mandatory in HealthDCAT-AP Release 5, but still not properly implemented throughout EU. Therefore, we keep it as optional for now.

meta:sssomMappings.$comment: A Beacon 'externalUrl' is broader than the specific dcat:relatedResource.
A Beacon 'externalUrl' can be represented through the generic FEGA/healthdcatap 'relatedResource'.

types.$comment: #! mix of HealthDCAT-AP 'http://publications.europa.eu/resource/authority/dataset-type' enums and EGA's

variableMeasured.$comment: #! This property tends to be more related to the Study than the dataset, at least in ISA and other standards.

previousVersion.description: The previous version of a resource in a lineage.

license.description: A legal document under which the resource is made available, giving official permission to do something with the resource.

hasPolicy.$comment: #! We may want to add the mapping to the 'policy_accession_id' not in this upper hasPolicy, but in an inner 'id' of the policy object itself.
